name: Katalon Test Automation API
what_it_does: Comprehensive test automation platform with REST API — enabling QA teams to create, run, and analyze web, mobile, desktop, and API tests from a single IDE, with AI-powered test maintenance, CI/CD integration, cross-browser and cross-device execution, and test result analytics.
use_cases: Triggering test suite execution in CI/CD pipelines via Katalon REST API
Querying test run history and failure analytics for QA metrics dashboards
Managing test configurations and environments programmatically via API
Integrating Katalon results with JIRA for automatic bug creation on test failure
Pulling execution reports for compliance and audit trail documentation
not_for: Pure performance/load testing (use k6 or Gatling)
Security testing and penetration testing workflows
Teams requiring open-source-only tools (Katalon Studio is freemium but not open-source)
best_when: A QA team needs a unified platform for web, mobile, and API testing with a low-code recorder, AI maintenance, and built-in reporting without maintaining a Selenium/Playwright infrastructure.
avoid_when: You're a developer team comfortable writing Playwright or Cypress directly, need open-source tooling only, or do primarily performance testing.

auth.notes: API key from Katalon Platform settings. Bearer token auth. Organization and project IDs required. No OAuth2. Webhook for test run completion and failure events. CLI runner (Katalon Runtime Engine) for local and CI execution. KatalonTestOps REST API for analytics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Katalon Studio: free IDE; TestOps: free up to 2,000 test minutes/month
pricing.notes: Vietnamese company (Ho Chi Minh City). Founded 2016. Raised $27M. 160,000+ users in 160 countries. Free IDE (Katalon Studio) + paid cloud (TestOps). Web, mobile, desktop, and API testing in one platform. AI self-healing and stale element fixes. Strong in enterprise QA teams in Asia-Pacific.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API (TestOps) for test runs, suites, results, and configurations. Bearer token auth. Organization and project IDs required. Good documentation. CLI runner for CI execution requires Runtime Engine license. Webhook for events. Multiple APIs: Studio API and TestOps API — use appropriate one for your workflow.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Test run reads idempotent. Suite reads idempotent. Result reads idempotent. Triggering new test run creates new record — not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate products: Katalon Studio (IDE) and Katalon TestOps (cloud) — separate APIs
Runtime Engine license required for CI/CD CLI execution
Organization and project IDs both required in API requests
AI self-healing is IDE-side feature — not available via API trigger
Vietnamese company — support hours may have timezone considerations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. Bearer token auth. Test scripts may contain sensitive app credentials — secure Katalon secret management. Runtime Engine deployed locally or in CI with API key — rotate regularly.
